signals.title: NZD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Energy Risk
signals.text: NZD 1W avg +1.425% (n=4) vs JPY -0.489% (n=7) creates a 1.915% G10 spread that faces immediate pressure from surging energy prices and Middle East tensions. Surging natural gas and crude oil prices (+4.46%) typically weigh on the energy-importing JPY, while the NZD COT net short of -29,582 (15.2% long) provides fuel for a short-squeeze if risk appetite holds. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul is the catalyst for determining if the JPY's yield disadvantage (266bp vs USD) will be addressed.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Trade Friction + COT Positioning Risk
signals.text: CAD COT net short -93,515 (20.6% long) leaves the pair vulnerable to a short-squeeze as Trump threatens new Canadian tariffs linked to wildfire smoke. While the +133bp OIS carry advantage favors USD, the extreme CAD short positioning and a -1.92σ correlation break with WTI oil suggest the pair is decoupled from energy tailwinds. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) is the catalyst for resolving the divergence between trade risks and restrictive real rates (+0.19%).

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Policy Divergence + CESI Underperformance
signals.text: EUR COT net short -74,637 (33.3% long) aligns with a strong negative CESI (-16 net misses) to maintain downside pressure toward the 1.1446 Friday close. The +146bp OIS carry advantage for USD is reinforced by the Fed's restrictive real rate (+1.47%) compared to the ECB's near-neutral stance (+0.03%). The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 Jul is the catalyst for confirming the hold bias amid surging energy-driven inflation risks.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Risk + Positioning Crowding
signals.text: GBP COT net long +28,278 (64.8% long) creates an unwind risk as GBP/USD IV (11.0%) shows an elevated +4.2% premium over realized volatility (HV30 6.8%). Despite a neutral nominal carry (0bp), the GBP real rate of +0.78% is significantly lower than the USD real rate of +1.47%, suggesting the long position is fundamentally stretched. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 30 Jul is the catalyst for testing the sustainability of the current long bias.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Retail Sentiment Divergence + CESI Weakness
signals.text: AUD COT net long +21,946 (63.2% long) faces a contrarian headwind from 67% retail short positioning and a strong negative CESI of -13 misses. While the -71bp OIS carry advantage favors AUD, persistent data disappointments and a bull flattener yield curve regime limit the upside potential from the 0.6985 Friday close. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug is the catalyst for assessing if the 4.35% hold bias remains appropriate.
